EXHIBIT A TO DEFENDANTS SUPPLEMENTAL DECLARATION OF RAYMOND TANG IN SUPPORT OF MOTIONT TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT JUDGMENT (TRANSACTION ID # 16052071) FILED BY DEFENDANT TANG, RAYMOND

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT RAYMOND TANG'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT, DEFAULT JUDGMENT; MOTION FOR CONSIDERATION TO REINSTATE DEFENDANTS ANSWER, THE COURT GRANTS DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE THE DEFAULT AND DEFAULT JUDGMENT ENTERED AGAINST DEFENDANT RAYMOND TANG. THE COURT FINDS THAT THE JUDGMENT IN THIS CASE IS VOID UNDER CCP SEC. 473(D). ALTERNATIVELY, THE COURT FINDS THAT THE ACTIONS OF DEFENDANT'S COUNSEL CREATED AN INTOLERABLE SITUATION (FILING AN ANSWER IN THE CASE WITHOUT THE DEFENDANT'S KNOWLEDGE). THE COURT FINDS THERE WAS INTRINSIC MISTAKE. DEFENDANT SHALL FILE HIS ANSWER WITHIN 10 DAYS. PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IS DENIED. DEFENDANT SHALL PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER TO THE COURT FOR SIGNATURE. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: SEAN KANE; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR #5976 (302/HK)

Matter argued by counsel. At 9:46 a.m., Defendant RAYMOND TANG is sworn and testifies under examination by the Court. Matter submitted by counsek. The Court vacates its tentative ruling, and orders as follows: As to DEFENDANT RAYMOND TANG's Motion To Set Aside Default, Default Judgment; Motion For Consideration To Reinstate Defendants Answer, the Court grants Defendant's motion to set aside the default and default judgment entered against Defendant Raymond Tang. The Court finds that the judgment in this case is void under CCP Sec. 473(D). Alternatively, the Court finds that the actions of defendant's counsel created an intolerable situation (filing an answer in the case without the defendant's knowledge). The Court finds there was intrinsic mistake. Defendant shall file his answer within 10 days. Plaintiff's request for sanctions is denied. Defendant shall prepare and submit a proposed order to the Court for signature. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976 (302/HK)
